title: Radiologic Technologist

rationales.taskAutomation: Image-quality QC and standard positioning-protocol documentation carry a modest digital share; operating imaging equipment and positioning patients remain hands-on.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI image-quality and dose-optimization tools are credible and improving, increasingly embedded in newer imaging systems.
rationales.adoption: Larger hospital imaging departments are adopting AI dose-optimization and QC tools; broader adoption is still building.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can flag image-quality issues and manage worklist prioritization, but positioning and operating imaging equipment stay with the technologist.

tasksGrowing: Complex imaging protocols and modality specialization
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2034 (Radiologic Technologists and Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI dose-optimization and image-QC tools are improving fast, but positioning a patient correctly for a diagnostic-quality scan is still a manual skill performed one patient at a time. Cross-training into CT and MRI modalities offers the clearest advancement path.
